title: arenaria aggregata subsp. tetraquetra
intro: Arenaria aggregata subsp. tetraquetra, also known as Four-angled Sandwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It is a low-growing, succulent plant that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin problems. The plant has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Arenaria aggregata subsp. tetraquetra is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat conditions like diarrhea and eczema.
